commit.message:
test(w69): fun + walk + content deep tests (~60 tests) (#587)

## Summary

Add deep integration tests for three feature-gated crates covering ~62
tests total.

### tokmd-fun (20 tests)
- OBJ rendering: determinism, structural invariants (vertex/face counts
scale linearly), face index validity, quad faces, name sanitization,
input order preservation
- Edge cases: zero-height buildings, all-zero dimensions, negative
coordinates, single-building eco-label proxy
- MIDI generation: determinism, empty notes, zero tempo clamping,
channel clamping, simultaneous notes, overlapping notes, high tempo

### tokmd-walk (20 tests)
- File discovery: flat dirs, nested dirs, relative paths, empty dirs
- Max-file limits: zero returns empty, max-one, exceeds file count
- Deterministic ordering across calls
- Hidden file handling (dotfiles)
- License/metadata candidate detection: standard licenses, manifests,
case-insensitive, sorting, determinism
- file_size: exact bytes, empty files, missing file errors, determinism

### tokmd-content (22 tests)
- Entropy: empty, uniform, 2-value (~1 bit), max (~8 bits), determinism
- Tag counting: basic detection, multiple occurrences, case-insensitive,
empty edge cases
- BLAKE3 hashing: determinism, hex format, collision resistance
- Text detection: ASCII, UTF-8, null bytes (binary), empty
- File reading: read_head limits, read_head_tail, read_lines zero max,
hash_file consistency
- Complexity: analyze_functions (empty, Rust, Python), cyclomatic
(empty, branching), cognitive, nesting depth

All tests are deterministic and pass on the current main branch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

| File | Lines |

Handle partial LLM answers as drafts, suppress disclaimers (#54)

* Initial plan

* Fix AI returning NO_INFORMATION despite relevant context by relaxing system prompt

The system prompt was too restrictive, requiring answers to be "fully
supported" before the model would generate a reply. This caused the LLM
to return NO_INFORMATION even when vector confidence was high (e.g. 81%)
and the retrieved context was clearly relevant.

Updated the prompt in both sandbox and webhook reply routes to:
- Allow partial answers with lower confidence scores
- Only return NO_INFORMATION when context is completely unrelated
- Explicitly instruct no markdown fences in output

Co-authored-by: harshithpabbati <43822585+harshithpabbati@users.noreply.github.com>

* Handle partial LLM answers as drafts with visual indicator

Introduce a PARTIAL status in the grounded answer agent prompt so the
LLM can distinguish between full answers, partial answers, and no
information. Key changes:

- System prompt now instructs the model to return status "PARTIAL" when
  context covers some but not all of the question, and to never include
  apologies or disclaimers about missing information.
- Webhook reply handler: partial answers are always saved as drafts
  (never auto-sent), preserving the AI content for agent review.
- Sandbox API: returns a `partial` boolean flag in the response.
- Sandbox UI: shows an amber "Partial Answer — Needs Review" banner
  when the reply is partial, explaining it would be saved as a draft.

fix: downgrade SignalR.Client to 9.x and simplify Blazor routing

- Root cause: Microsoft.AspNetCore.SignalR.Client 10.0.3 was incompatible
  with .NET 9 runtime — the MessagePack blazorpack protocol's
  IInvocationBinder.GetTarget(ReadOnlySpan<byte>) method doesn't exist in
  the 9.x server assemblies, causing immediate circuit disconnect.
- Downgraded to SignalR.Client 9.0.6 to match the .NET 9 target framework.
- Simplified Routes.razor to always use RouteView (no AuthorizeRouteView),
  since page-level auth gating is handled at the API layer.
- Updated Program.cs auth check to skip placeholder ClientId values.

fix: remediate code review findings across type safety, DRY, and resource management (#16)

* fix: remediate code review findings across type safety, DRY, and resource management

Address all findings from code review and Copilot review:

- Extract shared error formatting module (DRY for sany.ts/tlc.ts)
- Type server parameter as McpServer instead of any
- Convert JAR extraction to async with fs.promises.* (no busy-wait, no event loop blocking)
- Add LRU cache with bounded size and on-disk cleanup for JAR entries
- Cache knowledge base at HTTP startup for per-request reuse
- Discriminate fatal vs non-fatal HTTP errors (no crash on ECONNRESET)
- Consolidate duplicated SVG parsing in RenderService
- Guard SVG scaling against zero dimensions to prevent Infinity/NaN
- Guard CLI argument parsing against missing values
- Replace execSync with execFileSync + 5s timeout
- Read MCP server version from package.json dynamically
- Move @napi-rs/canvas to optionalDependencies with graceful degradation
- Align all version references to 1.0.0
- Change LRUCache eviction logging to stderr to avoid corrupting MCP stdio stream
- Stabilize flaky process-runner timeout test for CI

- Use iterator done flag in LRUCache.set() to handle undefined/falsy keys
- Move error handler declarations before app.listen() to avoid TDZ
- Extract requireValue() helper to detect flags passed as values in CLI
- Fix cache eviction to remove parent directory for file entries

Add 100 random space events system

- 40 negative events: meteor showers, solar flares, pirate raids, EMP pulses, etc.
- 40 positive events: stellar bonuses, OKR rain, repair nanobots, production boosts, etc.
- 20 neutral events: space whale sightings, alien broadcasts, cosmic shifts, etc.

- Introduced guidelines for calculating derived state during rendering to avoid redundant renders.

- Highlighted the importance of authenticating server actions like API routes to prevent unauthorized access.
- Implemented cross-request LRU caching for shared data across sequential requests.
- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.
- Advised avoiding duplicate serialization in RSC props to reduce network payload.
- Encouraged parallel data fetching with component composition to eliminate server-side waterfalls.

* Add best practices for React and server-side development

- Introduced guidelines for calculating derived state during rendering to avoid redundant renders.
- Added recommendations for subscribing to derived state to reduce re-render frequency.
- Emphasized the use of functional setState updates to prevent stale closures and unnecessary callback recreations.
- Suggested lazy state initialization for expensive initial values to optimize performance.
- Provided guidance on extracting default non-primitive parameter values from memoized components to restore memoization.
- Encouraged extracting expensive work into memoized components to enable early returns.
- Recommended placing interaction logic in event handlers to avoid unnecessary effect re-runs.
- Advised against wrapping simple expressions in useMemo to prevent wasted computation.
- Promoted the use of transitions for non-urgent updates to maintain UI responsiveness.
- Suggested using useRef for transient values to avoid unnecessary re-renders.
- Introduced the use of after() for non-blocking operations in server actions.
- Highlighted the importance of authenticating server actions like API routes to prevent unauthorized access.
- Implemented cross-request LRU caching for shared data across sequential requests.
- Recommended per-request deduplication with React.cache() for efficient data fetching.
- Advised avoiding duplicate serialization in RSC props to reduce network payload.
- Encouraged parallel data fetching with component composition to eliminate server-side waterfalls.
- Suggested minimizing serialization at RSC boundaries to reduce data transfer size.
- Added a new skill for reviewing UI code against Web Interface Guidelines compliance.

commit.message:
fix: respond to interaction before updating paradigms

All PairwiseView handlers (_record, _assign_special, undo) and
RateSelect callback now call interaction.response.edit_message
BEFORE _update_paradigms. This prevents Discord's 3-second
interaction timeout when paradigm messages take too long to edit.

When the demo user already exists but was created with a different
password (e.g. prior session, env change), register() is silently
skipped and login() fails because the stored bcrypt hash doesn't match.

Fix: when register() raises AuthError (email_taken), call
force_reset_password() to update the hash before attempting login.
The new force_reset_password() function is intentionally minimal and
internal — only called by demo_login.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
net.vschannel: chunk large TLS payloads to fix 16KB EncryptMessage crash on Windows (#26681)

* fix(vschannel): chunk large TLS request payloads on Windows

Split request payloads into cbMaximumMessage-sized chunks before EncryptMessage and send each encrypted record fully to avoid large-body crashes in Schannel path.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(tools): add vschannel 16kb httpbin probe script

Add a reproducible probe script that posts payloads around the 16KB boundary to https://httpbin.org/post and supports before/after expectation modes for regression verification.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style(tools): vfmt vschannel_16kb_httpbin_probe.v

* refactor(tools): apply Copilot suggestions to vschannel probe

- use os.new_process instead of os.execute to avoid shell escaping
  issues on Windows and separate stdout/stderr capture
- add explicit 'none' branch in expected_success_for_mode to make
  intent clear and guard against future unexpected mode values

* fix(vschannel): cast send result before DWORD accumulation
